summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json line 31 contains an inlined Node script (invoked as a lifecycle command) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/identity/env data, and POSTs it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The scanner.js module repeats the pattern: at lines 4-9 it pulls fs/os/https/child_process; at line 233 it execSyncs `whoami`; at lines 234/246 it captures os.hostname() and os.platform(); at line 222 it reads process.env; and at lines 142-161 it issues https.request/https.get calls to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io destination. The destination is a GitHub Pages site under an unrelated personal account, not a publisher-owned endpoint, and the package's stated purpose ("deployment key auditor") does not require shipping host identifiers and environment variables off-host. The combination of a hardcoded non-publisher C2, system reconnaissance (whoami + hostname + platform + cwd), env-var capture, and lifecycle-time HTTP POST is an unambiguous credential/host-recon exfiltration shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
